summary: Set 093 Session 2 (set-terminal): the Work Explorer module-row interaction surface, built on S1's persistent Plan/Session-sets child nodes. Shipped: (1) a module-ROW action strip — a hover/focus-revealed role=toolbar (secondary Tab-stop with internal Arrow/Home/End roving, Escape back to the row) carrying AI Plan / Import Plan / Open Plan / AI Sets, rendered for declared+pseudo kinds only (fallback shows none; its undeclared-slug warning owns remediation), plus Assign… on the pseudo Unassigned module; the toolbar buttons sit OUTSIDE the treeitem's aria-labelledby (=header) so they never pollute the tree name (amendment 1). (2) The same actions mirrored in a module-row context menu (new showModuleContextMenu message) and the Command Palette (added dabbler.openModulePlan for the Open Plan mirror; AI Plan/Import/AI Sets already had dabbler.importPlan/generateSessionSetPrompt). (3) QuickPick retirement on click paths: row/context invocations carry the module via pickModuleForAuthoring(root, ui, {preselectedSlug}); a new {kind:'unknown-module'} outcome fails loud on a stale slug (never a silent repo-level fallback); the palette commands keep their QuickPick. (4) Assign legacy sets to module…: a two-phase, ATOMIC, format-preserving module: stamp into each chosen unstamped set's spec.md config block — a col-0 splice at the yaml-fence top, target validated against the CURRENT manifest at write time, never writes default/pseudo, already-same-target is a no-op. A routed architecture ruling (opus tier 3, s2-targeting-seam-architecture.json) settled the D1-D4 seam design. Protocol: new moduleAction/showModuleContextMenu webview messages (closed enum + module identity, NOT via executeCommand/COMMAND_ALLOWLIST); a pure narrowModuleAction/narrowModuleIdentity strictly drops malformed identity (no coercion); a pure dispatchModuleAction maps each action to its handler (Layer-2 tested). Full suite GREEN: pytest 2922 passed/6 skipped, extension unit 1467 passing, Playwright Layer 3 24 passed, tsc clean; live dogfood 17/17 (all four strip actions end-to-end, no QuickPick). No Marketplace publish (single release boundary after Set 094).
verification_notes: Cross-provider verification (gpt-5-6, anthropic excluded) ran 11 rounds — an exceptionally deep adversarial drill of the operator-file-mutation writer. R1 (protocol fail-open: null slug coerced to repo-level, unknown kinds mapped to declared) → fixed via strict pure narrowing. R2 (unterminated-fence borrowing a later fence; Open Plan lacked a palette mirror) → bounded fence search + dabbler.openModulePlan. R3 (exact byte-for-byte post-write guard; missing never-hide-work regrouping test; dogfood didn't drive the strip actions) → all added. R4/R5 (rollback honesty; non-transactional partial write) and R6 (byte-diff guard broke on model:-first keys → deterministic recompute-compare) and R7 (rollback could clobber a concurrent edit; pointer-focus broke the toolbar anchor) → all fixed with regressions. R8 (manifest TOCTOU at write time), R9 (nested/block-scalar module: false noop → parsed top-level check; Open Plan palette re-pick → carry resolved module; and the write-safety class resolved structurally by making the writer ATOMIC: temp → verify → rename, which removes the rollback-vs-preserve dilemma entirely), R10 (residual pre-rename target re-check), R11 (unique per-operation staging path removing the shared-temp collision) → all fixed with regressions. Every material Critical/Major was fixed in-flight and re-verified. R11's RESIDUAL — serializing assignments across SEPARATE VS Code instances via OS-level file-locking — was OPERATOR-ADJUDICATED as adjudicated-minor: unwinnable in portable Node fs (no atomic conditional-replace), disproportionate for a single-line stamp, practical probability ~nil (two instances stamping the same unassigned set to different modules in the same microsecond). Per the Set 086 severity-gated-stop rule and the operator's explicit 'Accept & close' adjudication, the loop stopped at round 11 with the material class fully closed. Closed via --manual-verify (operator attestation); raw round artifacts s2-verification*.md / s2-issues*.json are immutable records.
path_aware_critique: advisory — pull_critique (google gemini-2.5-pro + openai gpt-5.4, 2 distinct providers) both returned VERIFIED with 0 findings, independently corroborating the final atomic-writer tree.

reason.specifics: Set 093 is the last session of this set. Routed next-set analysis (gemini-2.5-pro, s2-next-set-analysis.json) recommends Set 094 — the release-boundary set for the 091-094 Work Explorer redesign — under claude-code/anthropic/Opus/medium: it reuses the same host/webview/protocol surfaces (warm context) and is the single Marketplace publish for the group.
